California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
September 19, l-990
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-9 0-5 3I
OUESTIONS
financial 
interests of Regent Khachigian,
Dear Mr. Morrison:
This is in response to your l-etter of August l'6, 1990,
seeking advice regaiding the responsibi lities 
and duties under the
conflilt 
of interest provisions of the Political 
Reform Act (rrthe
Act'r) 1 of l"Ieredith J. Khachigian, a member of the Board of Regents
of the UniversitY of California.
cary Morrison
Deputy General Counsel
University of CaL ifornia
300 Lakeside Drive
oakland, CA 94612
In light of the
described belosr:
1 Gor.trr."nt code Sections 81ooo-91015'
are to the Government code.
(1) May Regent Khachigian participate in the deliberations
ana votes o? tn" site seleition task force in connection with
iecornmenaations it makes to the President of the University who
wilI, in turn, make a recommendation to the Board?
(2) May Regent Khachigian participate in decisions by the
Board'to nairow-the list oi sitLs and to choose the final site?
CONCLUSIONS
(1) Regent Khachigian may partj-cj-pate in the deliberations
a.ra .rJI6= oi tn" site ielection- task force in connection with
ie"ormnenaations it nakes to the President of the Universi.ty who
All section references
428 J Street, Suite 8OO a P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 . (916)322-5660


3.
Our Fi■e No. A-90-538
Page 2
will, in turn, make a reconmendation to the Board, because the
owner of the affected property has not been a source of income to
the Regent during the 12 months preceding such participation.
(2) Reqent Khachigian rnay participate in decisions by the
Board to narrow the list of sites and to choose the final site
because the owner of the affected property has not been a source
of income to the Regent during the 12 nonths preceding such
participation.
■.
FACTS
Meredith J. Khachigian is a nember of the Board of Regents of
the University of california (the rrBoardrr) .
In November 1988, the Board authorized university of
california President, David P. Gardner, to begin the process
of planning for possible ne!, campuses of the University.
In March 1989, President Gardner appointed a site selection
task force (the tttask forcerr) made up of Regent Khachigian,
two other members of the Board, and six members of the
university facul-ty and staff. 
The task force is charged with
advising the President on the devetopment and application of
crj.terj,a and nethodotogy for choosing new canpus sites. 
The
President considers the recomrnendations of the task force in
rnaking his recornmendations to the Board, which nakes the
fina] decisions.
In order to avoid any appearance of conflict of interest in
the site selection process, The Regents adopted special
financial disclosure requirements that before any actj.on is
taken by the Board to narro\./ the list of potential sites,
nembers of the Board disclose any economic or personal
interest they or members of their families rnay have related
to identified potential sites. 
The first 
such disclosure $/as
due in connection with the July 19 , L99o, neeting of the
Board to consider reducing to eight the sites under
consideration in the central region of the state. 
In early
Ju1y, in the process of naking this disclosure, Regent
rfra-higian was informed that a portion of one of the eight
sites was owned by Mr. Richard Gunner. Regent Khachigi'an's
husband, Kenneth, an attorney, had once performed legal
services as described below for Mr. Gunner on behalf of the
Gunner Ranch (hereafter rrcunnerrr) .
In January 1989, prior to Regent Khachigian's aPpointment to
the task force, and in connection with natters unrel'ated to
the university of california, 
Gunner contacted Mr. Khachigian
regarding advice on infrastructure development in the
vi6inity-ot 
property he ol^Ined in l{adera county--one parcel of
which is now among the eight sites referenced above.
4.
5.
2.


6.
Our Fi■e No. A-90-538
Page 3
7.
on April 20, 1989, Mr. Khachigian received a retainer fee of
rnore than S5oo fron cunner. Thereafter, Mr. Khachigian
performed 1ega1 services for cunner related to their
discussion in January. These legal services were
dj.scontinued as of JuIy 6, 1989, and no professional fees
were received by litr. Khachigian subsequent to April 20.
Holrever, Mr. Khachigian did receive reirnbursement for direct
out-of-pocket expenses in the sun of $523.88 on June 7, L989,
and in the sum of 9263.92 on August 7, 19a9. The August 7
pa)rnent was the final reinbursement of Mr. Khachigian by
cunner. Regent Khachigian has a community property interest
of less than 9250 in the August 7 pa)rment and of more than
$250 in the June 7 pa1,'ment as well as the April 20 fee. 
No
income of any kind has been received by Mr. Khachigian frorn
Gunner during the last 12 rnonths. Neither Mr. Khachigian nor
Regent Khachigian has engaged in any other contracts or
business transactions with Gunner.
At a1f times during the task force's deliberatj.ons and until
July 11, 1990, the sites bej.ng considered by the task force
were presented to its mernbers by code designations. So' for
example, the site which includes the Gunner property was
never referred to by any of its owners' names, but as uD 1.
At the June 8, L99o, task force meeting--at which sone 20
sites were brought j.n by the University staff with
recommendations to reduce the sefection to seven--the final
sites were stil1 referenced by code designations. on June 8,
the task force received and accePted the recommendations of
its staff.
on June 2l , L99o, President Gardner wrote to all the melnbers
of the Board, enclosing a Iist of counties in which the sites
were located in order that further infornation couLd be
provided to members who belj,eved these counties rnight
potentially require further disclosure. Regent and Mr.
Khachigian were out of the state on vacation fron July 1 to
JuIy 8, 1990. Upon returning, the two of them revieved
President cardner's comrnun j.cation, and on JuIy 11 replied by
facsirnile transmission that three counties woul-d require
further information--Tul-are, Fresno and Madera. The
Khachigians have nurnerous relatives in TuLare and Fresno
counties, and lttr. Khachigian and previous (though no active)
clients in all three counties.
on July 11, 1990, Regent Khachigian made specific inquiries
to University personnel about the sites and e/as informed that
Mr. Gunner owned property in the site previously referred to
as MD 1. That was the first tine Regent Khachigian became
aware that a site under consideration by the task force and
the Board included real, property ohrned by her husband's
former client.
■0.  Due to a previous commitment′  Regent Khachigian did not
attend the 」u■y ■9′  1990′  meeting of the Board at which the
8.


Our Fi■e No. A-90-538
Page 4
Board voted to further consider the eight sites referred to
it, including the parcel owned or controlled by Gunner.
Thus, fron the tine Regent Khachigian learned of the
ownership by cunner, she has not participated in any decision
relating to new site selection. on July 18, L99o, based on
the foregoinq facts, your office rendered an opinion to
Regent rhachigian that she did not have a disqualifying
financial. interest and could participate fuIly in site
selection issues.
11. Absent any disqualifying financial interest, Regent
Khachigian would anticipate participating in the following
acti.vities of the task force and the Board:
fn late september or early october the nembers of the
task force plan to personally visit each of the eight
sites currently under consideratj.on. You anticipate
that the sites will be the subject of informal
discussion among members of the task force, but no
action wi1l, be taken or recommendation made at that
tine.
Coincident with the site visits described above, the
task force will hold a series of public forums in the
areas of the potential sites. 
The task force plans to
listen to persons in affected communitj,es; no action
wil-1 be taken or recomrnendation made by the task force
at that tine.
On November 9 , 199Q, the site selectj.on task force will
have a meeting to consj,der available inforrnation about
the eight sites under consideration. You anticipate
that each of the eight sites will then be subject to a
vote by Regent Khachigian and the members of the task
force as to whether it should be recommended to
President cardner, The task force will then forlrard the
results of its vote to President Gardner to use in
connection with his recornmendation to the Board of
Regents at the Novernber neeting, when the Board plans to
reduce the eight sites to tlro or three. In voting on
lrhether to forward the sites for further consideration
by President cardner, the task force will be infomed by
a nunber of reports fron outside consultants with
respect to the criteria applicable to narrowing the
sites at this stage. The professional, staff of
President Gardner will also offer its recommendations to
assist the site selection task force in its
considerations.
At its Novenber 1990 neeting the Board, including Regent
Khachigian, is scheduled to vote to narro$/ the eight
sites currently under consideration to two or three for
more intensive further consideration.
a.
b.
C.
d.


Our Fi■e No. A-90-538
Page 5
The current schedule ca1ls for the Board, including
Regent Khachigian, to vote to choose a site for a new
campus at its November L991 meeting after considering
environmental impact report(s) and additionaL detailed
data on the sites reroaining under consideration.
ANALYSI S
Section 87103 provides:
rrAn official 
has a financial interest in a decision
within the neani.ng of Section 87100 if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the
public Aenerally, on the officia] or a nember of his or
her inrnediate fanily or on:
(a) Any business entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect investment $rorth one
thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.
(b) Any real property in which the public official
has a direct or indirect interest worth one thousand
dollars ($1,000) or more.
(c) Any source of incone, other than gifts and
other than loans by a commercial lending institution in
the regnrlar course of business on terms available to the
public without regard to official status, aggregating
tlro hundred fifty dotlars ($250) or more in value
provided to, recej.ved by or promised to the public
official 
within 12 rnonths prior to the time when the
decision is made.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official 
is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
enployee, or holds any position of nanagernent.
(e) Any donor of, or any internediary or agent for
a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating two hundred
fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by, or prornised to the public official 
within
12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.
rrFor purposes of this section, indirect investment
or interest Deans any investrnent or interest owned by
the spouse or dependent child of a public official, 
by
an agent on behalf of a public official, 
or by a
busj.ness entity or trust in which the official, 
the
official's 
agents, spouse, and dependent children own
directly, indirectJ.y, or beneficially a 10-percent
interest or greater. tl
e.


Our Fi■e No. A-90-538
Page 6
only subsection (c) of section 87103 would seem to be
applicable to the facts stated above. However, Regent Khachiqian
has not received income, either directly or through her spouse,
from llr. Gunnar during the 12 uonth period preceding ttre future
Board activities 
in rrhich the Regent lrould be participating.
Thus, Mr. Gunnar would not be a disqualifying I'source of income[
for those activities.
since no disgualifying economic interest appears from the
facts stated in your letter as to future activities of the Board
of Regents, Regent Khachigian need not disgualify herself fron the
planned participation.
The foregoing advice is linited to the facts stated in your
letter as to future conduct of the Regent. As you stated in our
recent telephone conversation, you did not intend to seek our
advice as to past conduct. Furthermore, we do not advise with
respect to past conduct. (Regulation 18329(c).)
I trust this letter has provided you wi,th the guidance you
requested. For your convenience, a copy of your inconing letter
is enclosed.
ff you have any further questions regarding this natter
please contact ne at (916) 322-509]-.
Sincerely,
scott Hall-abrin
Acting General Counsel
Atil.
